disclaimer: Seed data compiled from public sources. Every row confirmed_by_counsel=false until ratified by immigration counsel. Not legal advice.

rules.notes: Employer must obtain a prevailing wage determination (PWD) from the DOL National Prevailing Wage Center (Form ETA-9141 via FLAG) before filing ETA Form 9089. NPWC centralized PWD issuance effective Jan 1, 2010.
PWD validity period is at least 90 days from determination date.
PWD valid no more than 1 year. To rely on the PWD the employer must file the ETA-9089 or begin the recruitment required by 656.17(e) within the validity period.

rules.value: NPWC issuing OEWS-based PERM PWDs for requests filed April 2026 (as of 2026-06-30), roughly a 2-3 month queue
rules.source_citation: DOL FLAG Processing Times, PWD (OEWS), as of June 30, 2026
rules.notes: Snapshot data; refreshed monthly by OFLC. Verified against the live flag.dol.gov page on 2026-07-06; the June 30, 2026 refresh advanced the queue from March 2026 (June 8, 2026 release, per Fragomen May/June 2026 alert) to April 2026.

rules.value: NPWC issuing non-OEWS (employer survey/CBA) PERM PWDs for requests filed April 2026 (as of 2026-06-30)
rules.source_citation: DOL FLAG Processing Times, PWD (non-OEWS), as of June 30, 2026
rules.notes: Snapshot data; verified against the live flag.dol.gov page on 2026-07-06 (June 30, 2026 refresh; prior June 8, 2026 release showed March 2026 requests).
All required recruitment must be conducted within 180 days before filing ETA-9089; mandatory steps must also be conducted at least 30 days before filing (30-180 day window).
Mandatory recruitment steps must end more than 30 days before filing. Exception: for professional occupations, one of the three additional steps may take place within 30 days of filing.
Job order placed with the State Workforce Agency serving the area of intended employment for a period of 30 days.
Two advertisements on two different Sundays in a newspaper of general circulation; a professional journal may substitute for one Sunday ad if the job requires experience and an advanced degree.

For professional occupations, 3 additional steps chosen from 10 alternatives (job fairs, employer website, job search website, on-campus recruiting, trade organizations, private firms, employee referral program, campus placement offices, local/ethnic newspapers, radio/TV ads).
Notice of Filing posted at the worksite for 10 consecutive business days, ending at least 30 days and no more than 180 days before filing (where no bargaining representative exists).
DOL charges no fee to file ETA Form 9089. Filed via the FLAG system (new Form 9089 in FLAG since June 1, 2023).

rules.value: Adjudicating applications filed June 2025 or earlier (as of 2026-06-30); average 403 calendar days for recent determinations
rules.source_citation: DOL FLAG Processing Times, PERM Analyst Review, as of June 30, 2026
rules.notes: Roughly 13 months from filing to analyst-review decision as of mid-2026, improved from the prior release (April 2025 queue / 501-day average as of 2026-05-31, per Fragomen). Snapshot; refreshed monthly. Verified against the live flag.dol.gov page on 2026-07-06 and corroborated by Envoy Global's July 2026 DOL processing update (June 2025 queue, 403-day average).

rules.value: Audit review: cases dated December 2025 or earlier (as of 2026-06-30); average 290 calendar days for recent audit determinations
rules.source_citation: DOL FLAG Processing Times, PERM Audit Review, as of June 30, 2026
rules.notes: Verified against the live flag.dol.gov page on 2026-07-06 (June 30, 2026 refresh; prior release showed November 2025 / 343 days as of 2026-05-31) and corroborated by Envoy Global's July 2026 update. Measurement basis of the audit queue date (original filing vs audit response receipt) not stated on the summary; treat month with caution.

Approved PERM labor certification is valid 180 calendar days from certification; Form I-140 must be filed with USCIS in support of the certification within that window or the certification expires. CONFIRMS hypothesis.

UNVERIFIED: effective_date/FR citation for the 1991 promulgation need counsel confirmation; the requirement text is verified current (2026-07-06, ecfr.gov mirrored at law.cornell.edu due to eCFR rate-limiting). Employment-based petitions requiring a job offer must include evidence the employer can pay the proffered wage from the priority date until the beneficiary obtains LPR status: annual reports, federal tax returns, or audited financial statements (statement from a financial officer acceptable for employers of 100+). Referenced by transitions.json preconditions (rule_ref i140-ability-to-pay-required).

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 106.2; USCIS Fee Schedule G-1055 (edition 05/29/26); 89 FR 6194
rules.notes: Unchanged as of the May 29, 2026 G-1055 edition.

rules.value: $600 standard; $300 for employers with 25 or fewer full-time-equivalent employees; $0 for nonprofits
rules.notes: Employer-paid surcharge on Form I-140 (and I-129) filings under the April 2024 fee rule.
SUPERSEDED. $2,805 applied to I-907 requests for I-140 from Feb 26, 2024 through Feb 28, 2026. Hypothesis value stale as of 2026-03-01.

rules.source_citation: FR Doc. 2026-00321, Adjustment to Premium Processing Fees (Jan 12, 2026)
rules.notes: CPI-U inflation adjustment (June 2023-June 2025, +5.72%). I-907 requests postmarked on or after March 1, 2026 must include $2,965 for I-140. Other premium tiers rose $1,685 to $1,780 and $1,965 to $2,075.

rules.source_citation: 8 USC 1356(u) (USCIS Stabilization Act); USCIS premium processing page
rules.notes: 15 BUSINESS days for I-140 classifications E11 (EB-1A), E12 (EB-1B), E21 advanced degree/exceptional ability with job offer, and EB-3 (E31/E32/EW3). Clock restarts after RFE response. CONFIRMS hypothesis for standard classifications only.

45 BUSINESS days for E13 (multinational executive/manager) and E21 National Interest Waiver. E13 premium began June 1, 2022; NIW premium fully phased in Jan 30, 2023. CORRECTS the blanket 15-business-day hypothesis for these two classifications.

rules.value: Date the DOL accepted the PERM labor certification application for processing (filing date of ETA-9089)
rules.notes: CONFIRMS hypothesis for labor-certification-based EB-2/EB-3 cases.

rules.value: For PERM-exempt classifications (EB-1, EB-2 NIW, Schedule A): the date USCIS receives the properly filed Form I-140
rules.notes: CONFIRMS hypothesis. Schedule A cases use the date the I-140 with uncertified ETA-9089 is received by USCIS.

rules.value: Worker retains the earliest priority date from any prior approved EB-1/EB-2/EB-3 I-140 for any subsequently filed EB petition (including with a new employer), unless the approval was revoked for fraud, willful misrepresentation, material error, or the labor certification was revoked/invalidated
rules.notes: CONFIRMS hypothesis. Retention does not require the original employer's cooperation once the I-140 is approved.

An approved I-140 is NOT automatically revoked by employer withdrawal or business termination occurring 180+ days after approval (or 180+ days after an associated I-485 was filed). Petition remains valid for priority-date retention, H-1B AC21 extensions, and 204(j) portability, though not as a standalone basis for a new I-485 without a qualifying job offer.

rules.source_citation: DOS Visa Bulletin (monthly), two-chart system since October 2015 bulletin
rules.notes: Chart A = Application Final Action Dates (when a visa number may be authorized/case approved); Chart B = Dates for Filing (earliest submission). CONFIRMS hypothesis.

rules.source_citation: USCIS Adjustment of Status Filing Charts page
rules.notes: USCIS announces within about a week of each monthly Visa Bulletin whether I-485 filings that month may use Dates for Filing or must use Final Action Dates. CONFIRMS hypothesis.

rules.value: Final Action Dates
rules.notes: For July 2026, all employment-based I-485 filings must use the Final Action Dates chart. Snapshot; changes monthly.

rules.value: Worldwide/Mexico/Philippines: Current; China: 2023-06-01; India: 2022-10-15 (retrogressed 2 months)
rules.notes: Monthly snapshot data.

rules.value: Worldwide: Current; China: 2021-09-01; India: Unavailable (annual/pro-rated limit reached; unavailable for remainder of FY2026)
rules.notes: EB-2 India shows 'U' through Sept 30, 2026; numbers become available again Oct 1, 2026 (FY2027). Monthly snapshot.
Monthly snapshot data.
Statutory floor of 140,000 EB visas per fiscal year plus any unused family-sponsored numbers from the prior year.

No single country may receive more than 7% of combined family+employment preference visas per year (source of India/China backlogs); INA 202(a)(5) allows exceeding it when numbers would otherwise go unused.

rules.value: EB-1, EB-2, EB-3: 28.6% of worldwide EB limit each (plus fall-down of unused numbers); EB-4 and EB-5: 7.1% each
rules.notes: Unused EB-1 falls to EB-2, unused EB-2 to EB-3.

I-485 may be filed together with (or while pending) the I-140 when a visa number is immediately available under the chart USCIS designates for that month. I-765 (EAD) and I-131 (AP) may be filed concurrently. CONFIRMS hypothesis; note July 2026 requires Final Action Dates chart.
Since Apr 1, 2024 the I-485 fee no longer bundles free EAD/AP. Child under 14 filing with parent: $950. Unchanged as of the May 29, 2026 G-1055 edition.
Form I-485 is not eligible for premium processing as of 2026-07-06.
Reduced I-765 fee for applicants filing with, or based on, a pending I-485. Standalone I-765: $520 paper / $470 online.
Form I-131 advance parole for a pending-I-485 applicant. No fee waiver for AOS-based AP.

rules.source_citation: Immigration Parole Fee Required by HR-1 Reconciliation Bill, FR Doc. 2025-19564 (Oct 16, 2025); P.L. 119-21; USCIS alert 'USCIS Implements New Immigration Parole Fee Required by H.R. 1'
rules.notes: The P.L. 119-21 (HR-1) immigration parole fee ($1,000 FY2025; $1,020 FY2026 per the Nov 19, 2025 inflation-adjustment notice, FR Doc. 2025-20304) does NOT apply to I-485 adjustment applicants re-entering the US on a valid advance parole tied to their pending I-485; DHS implementation guidance provides a fee exception, documented by presenting the I-485 receipt notice at the port of entry. Corroborated by Mintz (Nov 4, 2025) and Capitol Immigration Law Group (Nov 3, 2025) analyses of the FR notice. Resolves an item the original research pass could not confirm.
Adjustment applicants may apply for open-market employment authorization while the I-485 is pending, and may renew for as long as it remains pending (including during retrogression).

rules.source_citation: USCIS Policy Alert, Sept 27, 2023 (5-year EADs for c09 and other categories)
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED. From Sept 27, 2023 to Dec 4, 2025, initial and renewal (c)(9) EADs were issued with up to 5-year validity. EADs issued during that window keep their printed validity.

rules.source_citation: USCIS Policy Alert PA-2025 (Dec 4, 2025), Employment Authorization Validity; USCIS Policy Manual Vol. 10
rules.notes: Maximum validity for initial and renewal (c)(9) adjustment-applicant EADs cut from 5 years to 18 months. Applies to I-765 applications pending on, or filed on/after, Dec 5, 2025. Does not shorten already-issued cards.

rules.source_citation: 8 CFR 274a.13(d) (as amended, 89 FR 101208, Dec 13, 2024, permanent 540-day rule)
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED. Timely-filed (c)(9) renewals received up-to-540-day automatic extensions. Still honored ONLY for renewal I-765s filed before Oct 30, 2025 (per receipt notice) and certain TPS categories.

rules.source_citation: DHS Interim Final Rule, Removal of the Automatic Extension of Employment Authorization Documents, 90 FR (Oct 30, 2025); 8 CFR 274a.13(d)
rules.notes: No automatic extension for EAD renewal applications filed on/after Oct 30, 2025 (exceptions: renewals timely filed before that date, and TPS A12/C19 Federal Register notice extensions). CORRECTS the hypothesis that EADs remain seamlessly renewable: renewable, yes, but a gap in work authorization is now possible. Litigation: APA challenge by seven H-1B spouses in C.D. Cal. (filed Jan 2026, Judge David O. Carter); TRO application Feb 13, 2026 converted to a preliminary-injunction motion May 8, 2026; briefing completed with plaintiffs' reply June 4, 2026; hearing held June 24, 2026; NO ruling or injunction as of 2026-07-06 - rule remains in force. A parallel D.D.C. challenge (filed Apr 20, 2026) was voluntarily dismissed June 2, 2026.

USCIS accepts (c)(9) renewal filings up to 180 days before the current EAD expires. The 180-day recommendation was re-verified as current guidance on 2026-07-06 (restated in USCIS's Oct 30, 2025 news release 'DHS Ends Automatic Extension of Employment Authorization'). Precise first-effective date of the 180-day window remains unconfirmed (effective_date intentionally null). Critical planning window now that automatic extensions are eliminated.

Departure without advance parole is deemed abandonment of the pending I-485 - EXCEPT applicants maintaining valid H-1B/H-4 or L-1/L-2 (and V/K-3/4) status who resume that status on return with a valid visa.

rules.value: I-485 remains pending and is held until the priority date is again current under the Final Action Dates chart; it is not denied or rejected due to retrogression
rules.source_citation: USCIS Visa Availability and Priority Dates; USCIS Policy Manual Vol. 7
rules.notes: Longstanding administrative practice (date shown reflects the modern two-chart framework). A properly filed I-485 is preserved even if the date later retrogresses. CONFIRMS hypothesis.

rules.value: Priority date is not lost or reset by retrogression; it remains fixed by the underlying PERM/I-140
rules.notes: CONFIRMS hypothesis.

CONFIRMS hypothesis with 2025 caveats: still renewable for the life of the I-485, but new EADs max 18 months (since 2025-12-05) and no automatic extension for renewals filed on/after 2025-10-30, so gaps are possible.

rules.source_citation: INA 204(j), 8 USC 1154(j) (AC21 sec. 106(c), enacted Oct 17, 2000); 8 CFR 245.25
rules.notes: If the I-485 has been pending 180+ days and the I-140 is approved (or approvable and later approved), the worker may take a new permanent job offer in the same or a similar occupational classification - with the same employer, a new employer, or self-employment - and the I-140/I-485 remain valid. CONFIRMS hypothesis.
Confirmation of a bona fide job offer or portability request is made on Supplement J (no separate filing fee under the current fee schedule).

rules.value: Same occupational classification: same or essentially the same occupation as the I-140 job. Similar: shares essential qualities or resembles it in marked degree; USCIS looks at SOC codes, duties, required skills/experience, wages, and career-progression evidence
rules.source_citation: USCIS Policy Manual Vol. 7, Part E, Ch. 5; 8 CFR 245.25(b)
rules.notes: Promotions into related supervisory roles can qualify as career progression.

3-year H-1B extensions beyond the 6-year max for beneficiaries of an approved I-140 who cannot adjust because per-country/worldwide limits make a visa number unavailable. Available in 3-year increments until adjustment concludes.
1-year H-1B extensions beyond the 6-year max when a PERM application or I-140 was filed at least 365 days before the requested start (and remains pending/approved without a final denial).

rules.value: Form I-693 signed by a civil surgeon on or after 2023-11-01: valid indefinitely
rules.notes: SUPERSEDED effective June 11, 2025.

rules.value: Form I-693 (signed on/after 2023-11-01) is valid only for the specific application it was submitted with, and only while that application is pending; it loses validity if the application is denied or withdrawn
rules.source_citation: USCIS Policy Alert, June 11, 2025; USCIS Policy Manual Vol. 8, Part B, Ch. 4
rules.notes: A refiled I-485 requires a new medical exam. Forms signed before Nov 1, 2023 retain the old 2-years-from-signature validity.

rules.value: An immigrant visa may only be issued when the priority date is current under the Final Action Dates chart; NVC uses the Dates for Filing chart to determine when applicants may submit DS-260 and documents (documentarily complete)
rules.source_citation: DOS Visa Bulletin, section on use of the two charts
rules.notes: Consular-processing counterpart to the USCIS chart designation.

rules.source_citation: DOS Fees for Visa Services (employment-based immigrant visa application)
rules.notes: DS-260 employment-based IV application processing fee per applicant. $345 amount verified directly against the travel.state.gov Fees for Visa Services page on 2026-07-06 ('Employment-based applications (processed on the basis of an approved I-140 or I-526 petition): $345.00'). Effective date reflects the Sept 12, 2014 DOS fee-schedule change.
Paid online after immigrant visa issuance and before green card production for consular-processed immigrants.

rules.value: For CSPA age calculation, USCIS uses whichever Visa Bulletin chart (Dates for Filing or Final Action Dates) it designated for I-485 filing that month - a child's CSPA age can lock using the Dates for Filing chart when USCIS accepts that chart
rules.source_citation: USCIS Policy Alert, Feb 14, 2023; USCIS Policy Manual Vol. 7, Part A, Ch. 7
rules.notes: Relevant to derivative children of EB workers facing retrogression.

rules.value: PWD (ETA-9141) -> recruitment -> PERM (ETA-9089) -> certification -> I-140 within 180 days -> I-485 (concurrent or later, when priority date current under governing chart) or consular DS-260
rules.source_citation: USCIS Permanent Workers overview; 20 CFR 656; 8 CFR 204.5, 245.2
rules.notes: Reference row encoding the canonical EB-2/EB-3 PERM-based sequence for workflow validation.
